perturbation.original_text: Each Sponsor that has complied with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Participating Sponsor") may terminate the participation in the Offer of any Sponsor that has not satisfied the funding requirements contemplated by the Equity Commitment Letter or otherwise failed to comply with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Defaulting Sponsor"); provided, however, that such termination shall not affect any right of any Participating Sponsor as against such Defaulting Sponsor with respect to such failure to fund or any such other non-compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Sponsor that has complied with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Participating Sponsor") may terminate the participation in the Offer of any Sponsor that has not satisfied the funding requirements contemplated by the Equity Commitment Letter or otherwise failed to comply with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Defaulting Sponsor"); provided, however, that such termination *shall* affect any right of any Participating Sponsor as against such Defaulting Sponsor with respect to such failure to fund or any such other non-compliance.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "shall not" to "shall" creates a direct contradiction. Initially, termination of a Defaulting Sponsor did not affect the rights of a Participating Sponsor against them. Now, the termination *does* affect those rights, introducing uncertainty about the Participating Sponsor's recourse against a Defaulting Sponsor. This contradicts the purpose of the original clause, which intended to preserve recourse irrespective of termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Defaulting and Participating Sponsors. Each Sponsor that has complied with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Participating Sponsor") may terminate the participation in the Offer of any Sponsor that has not satisfied the funding requirements contemplated by the Equity Commitment Letter or otherwise failed to comply with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Defaulting Sponsor"); provided, however, that such termination shall affect any right of any Participating Sponsor as against such Defaulting Sponsor with respect to such failure to fund or any such other non-compliance. If the Participating Sponsors unanimously consent to proceed (and whether or not any Participating Sponsor elects to seek specific performance as against any Defaulting Sponsor in connection herewith), the amount of the commitment of each Defaulting Sponsor shall be offered, first, to the Participating Sponsors pro rata (based on the commitments of such Participating Sponsors), and, thereafter, as applicable, to one or more other co-investors, unanimously approved by the Participating Sponsors. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, from and after the time any Sponsor becomes a Defaulting Sponsor, the approval or consent of such Defaulting Sponsor shall not be required for any purpose hereunder; provided, however, that each Defaulting Sponsor that ultimately participates in the Offer (as a result of any Participating Sponsor electing to seek specific performance as against such Defaulting Sponsor) shall cease to be a "Defaulting Sponsor" and shall have approval and consent rights hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text. The original text stated that the termination of a defaulting sponsor's participation 'shall not affect any right of any Participating Sponsor'. The changed text states that such termination 'shall affect any right of any Participating Sponsor'. This is a direct contradiction, creating uncertainty and potentially nullifying existing rights. The contradictory language directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the contract, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition provided in 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified, and the provisions hereof may be waived, only by an agreement in writing signed by each of the Sponsors.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified, and the provisions hereof may be waived, only by an agreement in writing signed by a majority of the Sponsors.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "each of the Sponsors" to "a majority of the Sponsors" directly contradicts the original intent of requiring unanimous consent for amendments or waivers. This creates uncertainty because some sections of the contract assume unanimous consent, while this modified section allows for changes with only majority approval, leading to conflicts when unanimous consent is necessary as the contract was original intended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pre-Acceptance Period. If and to the extent any such decision would or could (or would or could be reasonably foreseeable to) adversely affect any Sponsor in any manner, each decision to be made by or on behalf of Purchaser with respect to the Offer (or any similar transaction), any agreements related thereto ("Related Agreements"), or any transaction contemplated by the Offer or any Related Agreement ("Contemplated Transactions" and, together with the Offer and Related Agreements, "Offer Matters"), or any Offer Matter or combination of Offer Matters, in any case, on and from the Effective Date and until the Acceptance Time ("Pre-Acceptance Period") shall require the unanimous consent of the Sponsors, including, without limitation, any (a) announcement of intention, commencement, amendment, supplement, termination, extension, modification, or waiver of or related to the Offer or any term or condition thereof, including, without limitation, with respect to any commencement date, initial offering period, offer price, type or form of consideration to be offered or paid, timing or other term of acceptance for payment or payment of any subject security, withdrawal right, expiration date, subsequent offering period, or top-up option, (b) waiver of any condition to or of the Offer or determination as to whether or not any such condition has been, is, or will be satisfied, (c) filing, dissemination, or disclosure requirement or offering document or tender offer material, including, without limitation, with respect to any filing or exhibit, schedule, attachment, amendment, or supplement thereto, (d) agreement, instrument, document, or matter related to any borrowing, incurrence of indebtedness, or equity, debt, or other financing, (e) litigation or other legal proceeding, including, without limitation, with respect to any stockholder list or security holder listing or poison pill, rights plan, or similar right, or derivative or other litigation or legal proceeding related to the Company's capitalization, (f) designation, nomination, appointment, or election of any person as a member of the board of directors of the Company, or (g) agreement, arrangement, or understanding with the Company or any manager, director, officer, employee, stockholder, partner, affiliate, associate, or related party of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the requirement for amending or waiving provisions from "each of the Sponsors" to "a majority of the Sponsors." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that certain decisions during the Pre-Acceptance Period, particularly those that could adversely affect any Sponsor, "shall require the unanimous consent of the Sponsors." Therefore, the change creates a direct and strong in-text contradiction as the amended text allows for changes with only a majority, while the contradicted text demands unanimity, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein or in any Management-Ownership Agreement to the contrary, each Continuing Sponsor may, at any time or from time to time in such Sponsor's sole discretion, cause Purchaser to distribute or otherwise transfer to such Sponsor all (but not less than all) of the amount of the Shares, if any, then owned by Purchaser equal to such Sponsor's pro rata share thereof (based on the Units then owned by all Sponsors) in redemption of or otherwise in exchange and as consideration for the Units then owned by such Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything herein or in any Management-Ownership Agreement to the contrary, each Continuing Sponsor may, only with unanimous consent from all other Continuing Sponsors, at any time or from time to time in such Sponsor's sole discretion, cause Purchaser to distribute or otherwise transfer to such Sponsor all (but not less than all) of the amount of the Shares, if any, then owned by Purchaser equal to such Sponsor's pro rata share thereof (based on the Units then owned by all Sponsors) in redemption of or otherwise in exchange and as consideration for the Units then owned by such Sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "only with unanimous consent from all other Continuing Sponsors" contradicts the original clause that allows each Continuing Sponsor to act in their "sole discretion." This introduces an in-text contradiction by now requiring unanimous consent where previously individual discretion was permitted for share distribution, affecting the Continuing Sponsor's ability to independently manage their investment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distribution, Redemption, and Other Transfer Rights. Notwithstanding anything herein or in any Management-Ownership Agreement to the contrary, each Continuing Sponsor may, only with unanimous consent from all other Continuing Sponsors, at any time or from time to time in such Sponsor's sole discretion, cause Purchaser to distribute or otherwise transfer to such Sponsor all (but not less than all) of the amount of the Shares, if any, then owned by Purchaser equal to such Sponsor's pro rata share thereof (based on the Units then owned by all Sponsors) in redemption of or otherwise in exchange and as consideration for the Units then owned by such Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows a Continuing Sponsor to act in their sole discretion. The changed text introduces a requirement for unanimous consent from all other Continuing Sponsors, directly contradicting the original provision. The contradicted text then restates the language from the changed text. This creates a direct conflict regarding the conditions under which a Continuing Sponsor can cause the Purchaser to distribute shares, changing it from individual discretion to requiring unanimous consent. This is an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
